title: Sylvester's Question on twin-width of sparse random graphs
statement_text: Is $\mathrm{tww}(G) = \Theta(\sqrt{e(G)})$ with high probability for sparse Erd\H{o}s-R\'enyi random graphs and random cubic graphs?
context_text: Sylvester asked whether the general upper bound $\mathrm{tww}(G) \leq (1+o(1))\sqrt{3m}$ (due to Ahn, Hendrey, Kim and Oum) is tight with high probability in the sparse regime. This question motivated the study of twin-width for random graphs with sub-logarithmic average degree.
paper_contribution: The answer is negative for random $d$-regular graphs with $d = o(\log n / \log\log n)$ and for $G(n,p)$ with $p = o(\log n / (n \log\log n))$; the answer is positive for $p = \Omega(\log n / n)$.
notes: Reference given only as [Syl] with no bibliographic year recoverable from the extracted text.
